name: Junsu
training_background: Experienced in both weight training and running; regularly runs 10 km and half marathon distances.
schedule_constraint: Can train up to 6 days per week, up to 90 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to keep both lifting and running; enjoys structured training; does not want to give up lower-body training.
dislikes: Does not want a plan that sacrifices either lifting or running completely.
must_reflect_constraints: Hypertrophy must be the first priority.
Running should be placed at a maintenance or support level.
Lower-body lifting and running fatigue should not conflict.
dangerous_plan_examples: Intervals or long runs the day after hard lower-body lifting.
Running volume high enough to interfere with hypertrophy recovery.

name: Yeongon
training_background: Recently started weight training; beginner level; low flexibility.
schedule_constraint: 3–4 days per week, around 60 minutes per session.
injury_or_limitation: No injury reported, but limited flexibility.
must_reflect_constraints: Must match a beginner level.
Should include mobility or warm-up considerations.
Running should stay minimal or secondary.
dangerous_plan_examples: Advanced split routines for experienced lifters.
Ignoring mobility limits with complex compound lifts.

name: Jewoo
training_background: Enjoys both weight training and running; running is mainly used for fat loss.
schedule_constraint: Around 5 days per week, 70–80 minutes per session.
preferences: Likes both lifting and running, but sees running mainly as a conditioning or fat-loss tool.
dislikes: Does not need performance-oriented running plans.
must_reflect_constraints: Running should be a fat-loss support tool, not a performance plan.
Weight training stimulus must stay sufficient.
The structure should protect muscle retention while dieting.
dangerous_plan_examples: A running performance improvement program.
Too much cardio causing muscle loss risk.
major_deduction_points: Poor balance between fat loss and muscle retention.

name: Yulgyeom
training_background: Beginner in weight training; running only occasionally as a hobby.
primary_goal: Build a sustainable gym habit and basic fitness
schedule_constraint: 3 days per week, around 60 minutes per session.
preferences: Mainly interested in gym training; okay with light occasional running.
dislikes: Does not want an overly complicated plan.
must_reflect_constraints: Structure should be simple and sustainable.
The plan should remain gym-centered.
Running should stay light and secondary.
dangerous_plan_examples: Too many different exercise types.
Excessive beginner frequency or volume.
major_deduction_points: Too many side elements vs core goal.

name: Seongjoon
training_background: More interested in running than weight training; does lifting mainly for aesthetics.
primary_goal: Improve or maintain running performance and enjoyment
schedule_constraint: About 5 days per week, 60–75 minutes per session.
preferences: Enjoys running more; wants lifting to be efficient and supportive.
dislikes: Does not want a bodybuilding-style plan to dominate the schedule.
must_reflect_constraints: Running should be the main focus.
Weight training should be supportive.
Lower-body lifting should not excessively harm running performance.
dangerous_plan_examples: A hypertrophy-focused 5–6 day bodybuilding split.
Hard lower-body lifting colliding with quality running.

name: Jeongwon
training_background: Consistently active; has completed a half marathon and also does regular weight training.
primary_goal: Sustainable long-term health and balanced hybrid training
secondary_goal: Maintain both running ability and general strength
schedule_constraint: 4–5 days per week, 60–80 minutes per session.
injury_or_limitation: Has a congenital limitation in the left upper limb, with reduced shoulder range of motion and weaker grip strength on that side. He can run normally and perform many gym exercises, but some bilateral barbell movements and overhead positions may be difficult or unstable.
preferences: Wants a balanced and realistic plan; wants to keep both running and gym training.
dislikes: Does not want a generic one-size-fits-all plan that ignores his physical limitation.
must_reflect_constraints: The left upper-limb limitation must be explicitly considered.
Exercises demanding strong bilateral symmetry or overhead stability should be modified.
Machine, dumbbell, unilateral, ROM-modified, or grip-assisted options should be suggested when needed.
dangerous_plan_examples: Unmodified barbell overhead press.
Heavy bilateral barbell routines that demand symmetry and stability.
Generic intermediate templates that ignore the limitation.

name: Soyeon
training_background: Almost no exercise experience; very underweight; carries a heavy bag regularly.
primary_goal: Build basic strength and muscle mass
schedule_constraint: 3 days per week, 45–60 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to start gym training safely.
dislikes: Should avoid overly intense or intimidating programs.
must_reflect_constraints: The plan must be fully beginner-friendly.
Exercise selection should consider lower-back discomfort.
The focus should remain on muscle gain and basic fitness, not extra conditioning.
dangerous_plan_examples: Aggressive heavy axial loading from the start.
Adding running and pushing total fatigue too high.

name: Minseo
secondary_goal: Improve lower-body and general fitness without worsening pain
schedule_constraint: 3–4 days per week, around 60 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to keep exercising but cautiously.
must_reflect_constraints: Lower-body exercise selection should be knee-aware.
Lower-body work should be modified, not deleted.
Cardio should also be knee-friendly.
dangerous_plan_examples: Jumping, impact-heavy running, repeated deep knee flexion.
High-volume lower-body work that ignores pain.

name: Yujin
training_background: Likes both weight training and running; previously trained a lot, but is currently inconsistent.
schedule_constraint: Wants to train 4 days per week, around 60 minutes per session, but adherence may be unstable.
dislikes: Does not benefit from unrealistic all-or-nothing plans.
must_reflect_constraints: The plan should be adherence-friendly.
Frequency and difficulty must be realistic.
A low-friction restart structure is preferred.
dangerous_plan_examples: 6+ days per week of hard training.
Designing around past fitness instead of current consistency.

name: Gaeun
training_background: Beginner; likes gym workouts and treadmill running.
primary_goal: Improve general fitness and build a healthy exercise habit
schedule_constraint: 3–4 days per week, 50–60 minutes per session.
injury_or_limitation: Frequent lower-back pain due to carrying a heavy bag.
preferences: Prefers indoor exercise and treadmill running.
dislikes: Likely to dislike plans centered on outdoor running or high-skill barbell movements.
must_reflect_constraints: Structure should be beginner-friendly.
Lower-back discomfort must be considered.
Indoor exercise and treadmill preference should be reflected.
dangerous_plan_examples: Heavy free-weight routines that load the lower back.
Outdoor-running-centered plans.
